Fortnite Season 6 theme REVEALED with new update? DJ skins, rumours, 'party' map change?
Fortnite Season 6 theme REVEALED with new update? DJ skins, rumours, 'party' map change?
0
Dom Peppiatt
24th September 2018
EPIC
Fortnite Cube and Season 6 NEWS: Epic Games New Battle Pass, Skins and release date info?
(Pic: EPIC)
Fortnite Season 6 - What happens next?
We've just seen a new update from Epic that's given us our first concrete tease of Fortnite Season 6.
"All great parties need a DJ," reads the tweet, featuring a picture of a llama decked out like a cyber DJ (think Deadmau5/Daft Punk).
"3 days to Season 6," the tweet concludes.
This is a hint - are we getting a party theme, a music theme? Is the lake going to turn into a massive party volcano?
Fortnite Season 6 countdown
Related Articles
Fallout 76 will always be an online game, Bethesda's Pete Hines confirms
Games with Gold September 2018: Xbox BIG News ahead of Major Nelson Free games reveal
Fortnite Starter Pack 3: Ace Skin Release Date LEAKED by PS4 Store as Wingman Skin leaves
DS
(Pic: DS)
Fortnite Season 6 is due to start soon - and ahead of the change from Season 5 to Season 6, we're expecting to see Epic tease the new content.
Where the Season 4/5 crossover saw a massive rift open in the sky, the 5/6 period has seen a massive mysterious cube enter the map and move around of its own accord.
Between this and some other rifts opening up here and there - damaging players with lightning bolts - it's safe to say we're well on the way to seeing more Season 6 changes occur in-game.
Below is our complete guide to all things Fortnite Season 6 - we'll update it as more information is revealed.
DS
(Pic: DS)
Fortnite Season 6 - When will it start?
Epic hasn't officially announced a start date for Season 6... yet.
on September 20
Epic is usually a little bit flexible with the dates new Seasons start on though, thanks to the work that goes into the backend of the game when the changeovers occur.
So depending on the weight of the work the developers need to put in, we could be seeing more (or less!) of a wait time.
Fortnite Skins: Update 5.3 LEAKED Skins and costmetics coming to the shop SOON
Take a look at some upcoming Fortnite cosmetics found in the latest Patch v5.3 files
1 / 17
DS
(Pic: DS)
Fortnite Season 6 - What's with the Cube?
As of last month, a giant purple Cube entered Fortnite and has been rolling around the map, confusing players as it goes. The bizarre item surely holds the clues to what's coming next in the game...
To understand the Cube, you need to understand Fortnite 'lore' so far. It began when a missile launched and tore a rift open in the sky. Then, from the rifts, new areas came to the game and a giant temple celebrating a tomato-based God appeared, too. The Enforcer skin dropped, which hinted at putting a stop to these space/time shenanigans, and the rift began to close.
But as it closed, it summoned a sentient Cube. The Cube - which punishes those that shoot at it with deadly bolts of purple lightning - moves every two hours towards an undisclosed destination. Datamined information suggests the Cube is en route to Loot Lake, but as of right now, its motivations and destination remain a mystery.
The Cube made its way to Loot Lake and melted, creating a big bouncy lake - and that's where we're at now.
DS
(Pic: DS)
Fortnite Season 6 - What is the theme?
We haven't been told what's going on with Season 6 just yet, but there are a few clues.
In a recent tease for the Capoeira emote, Epic seemed to let slip that a castle will be joining the game (you can just about see it in the background, very far away). This, paired with the 'Crazy Castle' spray introduced in the game as part of the Season 5 Battle Pass, suggests we may be getting a fantasy/Medieval theme.
That would continue the time-travel narrative Epic has introduced so far - maybe the Cube will zap the map back into the past?
Who knows at this point... Epic likes to tease and keep players on their toes, so we're excited to see what comes next.

Resident Evil 2 Remake: Claire Redfield and RE engine shines in latest Gamescom preview
Battlefield 5: Battle Royale mode, new single-player level TEASED in new Gamescom trailer
The Division 2: Ubisoft Reveals How They Settled On Washington D.C for Open-World sequel
DS
(Pic: DS)
Fortnite Season 6 - What Skins will be introduced?
Every new Season sees the introduction of new skins. At the time of writing, we've not had any leaks or datamines that reveal what sort of skins we'll be seeing as of the start of Season 6.
Maybe if we do go back in time, we can expect to see Medieval knights and dragon-themed skins. Maybe we can see mysterious Mayan or Aztec skins. Maybe Neanderthals. Who knows?
Perhaps the space/time Enforcer thing will be explored more, too - we'll update our gallery and include new information in this page as more infomation presents itself.
Fortnite 5.2 SKINS LEAKED: Update 5.20 Patch reveals NEW skins coming to shop very soon
Take a look at some upcoming Fortnite cosmetics found in the latest Patch v5.2 files
1 / 32
Fortnite Season 6 - Hats
Rumours abound that Fortnite will be getting hats soon, too. A set of leaks taken from datamined files of the newest build of the game suggests makeovers to older costumes could be coming to the game, supported by new custom headwear.
Data suggested that ???favorite_hat,??? ???favorite_vehicledeco,??? and ???favorite_mapmarker??? would be introduced to the game.
Coming off the back of the Tomato head that was introduced to the game recently, it would make sense to see more custom head items introduced - and for Epic it makes sense: more items to unlock means more rewards for players, and another revenue stream introduced to the game, too.
DS
(Pic: DS)
Fortnite Season 6 - Custom Battle Bus
In the datamine for patch 5.40, players have managed to determine that different Battle Buses will be coming to the game.
Thanks to a text field in the title, we learned Fortnite will:
"Randomly chooses between favorite Battle Buses (or all owned Battle Buses if nothing is marked as a favorite)"
One example we've seen so far is a Purple Battle bus (which we know about thanks to game files) though we've not seen it yet.
DS
(Pic: DS)
Fortnite Season 6 - Weapon Skins
The same patch revealed that we'll see a large number of high profile weapons get custom skin options, too.
This is something that's been requested heavily by the Fortnite community and is a great addition to the game - despite having a third-person view that obscures your weapon from your own view most of the time.
No skin examples have been leaked just yet, but we're expecting to hear more about them soon.
DS
(Pic: DS)
Fortnite Season 6 - New Consumables
Part of the version 5.40 datamine has revealed a new consumable items called 'Balloons'.
From the text supporting the item, it appears the consumable helps players gracefully fall, float and possibly fly - similar to the Hop Rocks item we've seen before.
'Rapid inflate balloons to help break your fall or soar to new heights!' reads the item description.
We're intrigued to see how this is going to work in-game.

Frequently Asked Questions
Are there additional resources for law enforcement to use when they receive the report of a critically missing child?
Where and why did AMBER Alert first start?
The AMBER Alert System began in 1996 when Dallas-Fort Worth broadcasters teamed with local police to develop an early warning system to help find abducted children.
AMBER stands for America's Missing: Broadcast Emergency Response and was created as a legacy to 9-year-old Amber Hagerman, who was kidnapped while riding her bicycle in Arlington, Texas, and then brutally murdered. Other states and communities soon set up their own AMBER plans as the idea was adopted across the nation.
How does it work?
Once law enforcement has determined that a child has been abducted and the abduction meets AMBER Alert criteria, law enforcement notifies broadcasters and state transportation officials. AMBER Alerts interrupt regular programming and are broadcast on radio and television and DOT highway signs. AMBER Alerts can also be re-disseminated through lottery, digital billboards, Internet Ad exchanges, Internet Service Providers, Internet search engines, as well as wireless devices such as mobile phones.
How effective has it been?
As of December 23, 2015 there have been 800 children rescued and returned specifically because of AMBER Alert.
AMBER Alerts also serve as deterrents to those who would prey upon our children. AMBER Alert cases have shown that some perpetrators release the abducted child after hearing the AMBER Alert.
Who is the National Coordinator for AMBER Alert and what is the Coordinator's role?
The Assistant Attorney General for the Office of Justice Programs, U.S. Department of Justice, serves as the National AMBER Alert Coordinator. The role of the National Coordinator is to facilitate the development of the AMBER network, support the development of state AMBER plans and efforts, help eliminate geographic gaps in AMBER networks, provide regional AMBER network coordination and provide guidance on criteria for issuing an AMBER Alert.
How does the AMBER Alert plan help children and families?
The establishment of AMBER Alert plans in all 50 states, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands and the expansion of the program into Indian Country and our northern and southern borders mark an important milestone in our efforts to prevent child abductions. No matter where a child is abducted, communities and law enforcement work together to recover missing children quickly and safely. As of December 23, 2015, 800 children have been recovered because of the AMBER Alert system. AMBER Alert is making a difference in saving children's lives.
What are the criteria for issuing AMBER Alerts?
Each state AMBER Alert plan includes its own criteria for issuing AMBER Alerts. The PROTECT Act, passed in 2003, which established the role of AMBER Alert Coordinator within the Department of Justice, calls for the Department of Justice to issue minimum standards or guidelines for AMBER Alerts that states can adopt voluntarily. The Department's Guidance on Criteria for Issuing AMBER Alerts is as follows:
There is reasonable belief by law enforcement that an abduction has occurred.
The law enforcement agency believes that the child is in imminent danger of serious bodily injury or death.
There is enough descriptive information about the victim and the abduction for law enforcement to issue an AMBER Alert to assist in the recovery of the child.
The abduction is of a child aged 17 years or younger.
The child's name and other critical data elements, including the Child Abduction flag, have been entered into the National Crime Information Center (NCIC) system.
Most states' guidelines adhere closely to the Department of Justice's recommended guidelines.
How are AMBER Alert appropriations spent?
To date, the Department of Justice has utilized appropriated funds to create an AMBER Alert communications network and provide the states and territories with training and technical assistance to develop and enhance their AMBER Alert plans.
Can AMBER Alerts be issued across state and jurisdictional lines?
Absolutely. When an AMBER Alert is issued an abductor may take the child outside the jurisdiction of the issuing law enforcement agency, crossing state boundaries. When that occurs, the AMBER Coordinator contacts the State AMBER Coordinator where the abducted child is believed to be and requests the AMBER Alert be extended into their jurisdiction. This is referred to as a Multi-state AMBER Alert activation.
Are AMBER Alerts issued for all missing children?
AMBER Alerts are issued for abducted children that meet the AMBER Alert criteria. AMBER Alert is only one tool that law enforcement can use to find abducted children. AMBER Alerts are used in the most serious cases that meet the AMBER criteria. Overuse of AMBER Alert could result in the public becoming desensitized to Alerts when they are issued.
What can I do if my child goes missing?
1-800-THE-LOST (1-800-843-5678)
http://www.missingkids.org/MissingChild
Are there additional resources for law enforcement to use when they receive the report of a critically missing child?
The Department of Justice launched an initiative in 2006 to train Child Abduction Response Teams (CART) nationwide which are designed to assist local law enforcement agencies when they respond to incidents of missing and abducted children. The teams include regional law enforcement investigators, forensic experts, AMBER Alert coordinators, search and rescue professionals, policy makers, crime intelligence analysts, victim service providers and other interagency resources.
AMBER Alerts are issued when a child abduction meets the specific AMBER Alert criteria, however, CART can be used for all missing children's cases. To date, there are 99 Operational Ready teams and 3799 team members trained representing 45 states, District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, the Bahamas, and Canada.
What is the Wireless Emergency Alert program?
http://www.missingkids.org/gethelpnow/amber/wea

Kaia Gerber
Cindy Crawford
10 of Kaia Gerber’s Absolute Best Hair and Makeup Moments
Yup. The mommy-shamers are in full force again and this time their target is the 51-year-old supermodel. Here’s what happened: A few days ago, Gerber, like the teenager she is, felt like taking a selfie, so she headed to the bathroom for its A-plus lighting. For the most part, the selfie looked fine. Gerber’s smokey makeup was on-point and her newly cut shag hair was perfectly tousled.
Where the controversy came in is with what Gerber was wearing and how she posed. In the photo, Gerber sported an oversized white bathrobe, which she draped loosely over her shoulders. Like people are under bathrobes, the up-and-coming model likely was nude, which is why the draped robe appeared to fall just shy of exposing her breasts.
@kaiagerber
Kaia Gerber’s New Shag Haircut Is Giving Us Serious Summer Vibes
Though no private parts were visible in Gerber’s photo, that didn’t stop the Internet’s mommy-shamers from coming down hard on her famous mom’s parenting choices. Considering Gerber’s age, a majority of the comments slammed Crawford for allowing a 15-year-old to take such a racy selfie.
“I love Kaia with all my heart and she is gorgeous, but she is too young for this type of photo,” one said.
“It was a matter of time… but I still hate to see this. Cindy — take the reins now before you can’t anymore,” another added.
“Is she seriously 15?!! How can her parents allow her to exploit herself in this way,” a person commented.
“Her parents don’t care,” another chimed. “Lack of good parenting results in this false glorification of the self… she doesn’t look like a 15-year-old girl. She looks like a woman, she teases like one, and she probably behaves like one. It’s all about the external image, an empty shell. They are craving for the wrong attention!”
While Gerber’s selfie is racier than we would typically expect from a 15-year-old, hardcore mommy-shaming is never the solution. Besides, it’s hard to believe that random opinions on the Internet know better for Gerber than Crawford herself.

The Good Doctor season two plot: What will it be about?
©
ABC/Eike Schrote
Season one's finale ended on a note of hope. We learnt that Dr Aaron Glassman's (Richard Schiff) inoperable brain cancer was actually a low-grade glioma that can be treated.
David Shore warned fans that Glassman is not in the clear yet.
"To be honest, people seem to die on TV way more than they die in real life," he said. "For your regular characters, it seems to be the go-to place.
"But Glassman's not out of the woods, and I think it's much more realistic and much more interesting and a greater storytelling opportunity to explore that journey of being sick.
"This is a show about medicine, and we have guest stars come in and get sick and get better and whatever. How do our people deal with that?
"How is Dr Glassman going to deal with his illness going forward? How is Dr Murphy going to deal with a sick mentor? What is that going to do to their relationship? What challenges is that going to present? I want to fully explore that."
Expect this storyline to be a key aspect of season two.

The Boston Celtics are a professional basketball team. They play their home games in the TD Garden in Boston, Massachusetts . They are a part of the National Basketball Association (NBA) and play in the Atlantic Division.
[1]
From 2008 to 2013, they were lead by the big three: Kevin Garnett , Paul Pierce and Rajon Rondo . But in July 2013, Garnett and Pierce were traded to the Brooklyn Nets , [2] and Rondo was traded to the Dallas Mavericks .
The rivalry between the Boston Celtics and Los Angeles Lakers is two most of the best franchises in NBA history. It has been called the NBA's best rivalry. [3] The two teams have met a record twelve times in the NBA Finals , starting with their first Finals meeting in 1959 . They would go on to win the league in the 1960s and the 1980s, playing each other six times in the 1960s and three times in the 1980s.
The rivalry had been less known since the retirements of Magic Johnson and Larry Bird in the early 1990s, but in 2008 it happened again when the Celtics and Lakers met in the Finals for the first time since 1987, with the Celtics winning the series in six games. They played once again in the 2010 NBA Finals which the Lakers won in seven games. The two teams have won the two highest numbers of championships, the Celtics 17, the Lakers 16; together, the 33 championships account for almost half of the 67 championships in NBA history.

Dallas police officer who fatally shot neighbor in his apartment is fired
Amber Guyger mistakenly entered Botham Jean’s apartment and said she thought she had encountered a burglar inside her home
Associated Press in Dallas
18.14 BST
18.23 BST
Shares
Dallas
Dallas: calls for justice after police officer kills man in his own home
Read more
Dallas police fired Officer Amber Guyger weeks after she fatally shot 26-year-old Botham Jean inside his own apartment on 6 September. Court records show Guyger said she thought she had encountered a burglar inside her own home.
Guyger was arrested on a preliminary charge of manslaughter days after the shooting. She is out on bond. Jean family attorneys and protesters called for her firing following the shooting.
Guyger was a four-year veteran. She told investigators that she had just ended a shift when she returned in uniform to the South Side Flats apartment complex.
Sign up for the US morning briefing
When she put her key in the apartment door, which was unlocked and slightly ajar, it opened, the affidavit said. Inside, the lights were off, and she saw a figure in the darkness that cast a large silhouette across the room, according to the officer’s account.
She called 911. When asked where she was, she returned to the front door to see she was in the wrong unit, according to the affidavit. The 911 tapes have not been released.
